summary:
**Claim**: The standard age group used to determine child mortality rate is 0–5 years.  

**Evidence**: - World Health Organization (WHO) and UNICEF universally define child mortality as deaths occurring among children under five years old. This includes all children born alive who die before completing their fifth year. - Global public health frameworks, including Sustainable Development Goal 3, rely on this metric to track progress in reducing early-life mortality and improving healthcare access.  

**Answer**: A
